name: Glazed Apple Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 McIntosh apples (about 1 1/2 lb., cored and cut into quarters)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine the McIntosh apples with water and lemon peel. Bring to a boil and simmer covered for 20 minutes or until tender.
2. Cook the mixture uncovered over moderately high heat, stirring frequently, until the water is almost evaporated. Force through a food mill into a bowl; allow to cool (or process in a food processor).
3. Butter a 9-inch springform pan and preheat oven to 350°F. In a large bowl, cream butter, sugar, and beat until light and fluffy.
4. Beat egg into the mixture and stir in apple puree. Sift flour, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and salt into a bowl; gently stir into apple mixture and pour batter into pan.
5. Peel, core, and halve Granny Smith apples lengthwise; cut crosswise into thin slices. Arrange apple slices decoratively in bunches on batter, pressing lightly into the batter.
6. Bake 1 1/4 hours or until a cake tester inserted in center comes out clean. Remove from oven to rack.
7. Melt jam in small saucepan over low heat; brush over top of cooled cake and let cool for 20 minutes. Loosen edges with a sharp knife, remove pan sides, and serve warm or at room temperature with whipped cream if desired.
